news.headline: What is really happening in northern Nigeria
news.summary: There is no US-led conspiracy or a ‘Christian genocide’. There is an insurgency that has adapted.

news.headline: Outrage after Israeli soldier desecrates statue of Jesus Christ in Lebanon
news.summary: A photo of an Israeli soldier smashing a statue of Jesus Christ in southern Lebanon has sparked widespread outrage.

news.headline: Oil prices surge amid mixed signals on US-Iran peace talks
news.summary: Brent crude rises more than 7 percent as Washington and Tehran offer conflicting accounts on ceasefire negotiations.

news.headline: What’s behind the US army’s decision to raise enlistment age to 42?
news.summary: Changes in US Army enlistment standards could be an effort to boost recruitment as numbers have lagged in recent years.

news.headline: Iran war live: Tehran slams US ‘piracy’ after ship seizure, vows response
news.summary: Attack comes hours after US President Donald Trump announced he is sending his team to Islamabad for possible talks.

news.headline: Outrage after photo shows Israeli soldier smashing Jesus statue in Lebanon
news.summary: Social media users condemn Western silence on attacks on religious symbols and sites by Israeli soldiers and settlers.

news.headline: US forces attack and seize Iranian ship Touska near Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: US Central Command has published a video said to show a guided-missile destroyer firing at an Iranian-flagged ship.

news.headline: Argentina’s Milei backs US-Israel war on Iran in Jerusalem visit
news.summary: Argentina’s president reaffirmed alignment with the US and Israel during a visit to Jerusalem, backing their war on Iran

news.headline: Schools, shops shut in northern Israel to protest the Lebanon ceasefire
news.summary: Shops and schools shut in northern Israel as residents protested a 10-day ceasefire with Lebanon.

news.headline: Protesters defy Berlin rain to call for end to global wars
news.summary: Hundreds protested in Berlin against the US-Israeli aggression on Iran, and Israeli attacks on Gaza and Lebanon.

news.headline: Who will shape the global agenda, the left or far right?
news.summary: Left-wing leaders meet in Barcelona, while far-right protesters march in Milan.

news.headline: With loss at Man City, have Arsenal lost their grip on the Premier League?
news.summary: Arsenal's loss to City and a woeful end-of-season run have now cost them their once-comfortable, top-of-the-table lead.

news.headline: Bulgaria election: Ex-President Radev’s party leads in early results
news.summary: Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria party takes strong lead in parliamentary election.

news.headline: Canadian PM says close economic ties with US have become a ‘weakness’
news.summary: Mark Carney says Canada must build economic ties with other countries amid shifting relationship with the United States.

news.headline: Trump says US seized Iranian ship trying to get past blockade near Hormuz
news.summary: Iran vows retaliation, accuses US of violating ceasefire after attack on cargo ship Touska.

news.headline: Salah scores in Merseyside derby as Liverpool win to boost European hopes
news.summary: Salah scores Liverpool's first goal, but Virgil van Dijk scores an injury-time winner against local rivals Everton.

news.headline: Israeli ministers celebrate re-establishment of Sa-Nur West Bank settlement
news.summary: Sa-Nur is one of four former West Bank settlements approved by the government two decades after settlers were evicted.

news.headline: Did the US misjudge Iran? A military strategist answers
news.summary: Strategist Bryan Clark examines shifting war aims, escalation traps, and whether Iran is now more dangerous to the US.

news.headline: Man City beat Arsenal 2-1, cut Premier League race to three points
news.summary: City edge the Gunners, thanks to a second-half Haaland winner, in a heated top-of-the-table league game in Manchester.

news.headline: Iran and Israel: From friends to enemies
news.summary: Israel and the US have been at war with Iran since Feb 28th. All sides have suffered, but it wasn’t always this way.

news.headline: UN chief strongly condemns killing of another peacekeeper in southern Lebanon
news.summary: A UN peacekeeper has been killed and three others injured after a UNIFIL patrol came under fire in southern Lebanon, the mission said on Saturday.

news.headline: UNICEF ‘outraged’ by killing of Gaza water truck drivers, urges investigation
news.summary: The UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) has raised alarm over the killing of two contracted workers delivering clean water to families in the Gaza Strip.

news.headline: World News in Brief: Myanmar amnesty, rising needs in Afghanistan, another power loss at Ukraine nuclear plant
news.summary: Authorities in Myanmar released the country’s ousted president from prison on Friday, along with some 4,000 other people, as part of an amnesty to mark the traditional New Year festival.

news.headline: ‘Slaughtered like goats’: Despair and abandonment in South Sudan
news.summary: Senior UN officials painted a sorry picture of South Sudan on Friday at the Security Council, describing political turmoil, rising violence, hunger and disease, amid budget cuts that are limiting the ability of the UN peacekeeping mission to protect civilians.

news.headline: As the ‘world court’ turns 80, Guterres says law must prevail over force
news.summary: The world is at a “moment of crisis” and countries must reaffirm commitment to international law amid rising violations and geopolitical tensions, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said at an event on Friday to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the International Court of Justice (ICJ).&nbs

news.headline: Record number of Rohingya refugees died at sea in 2025: UNHCR
news.summary: In 2025, nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal, making it the deadliest year on record in South and Southeast Asia, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, said on Friday.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins; Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon came into effect at midnight in Beirut, offering a pause in hostilities after weeks of fighting. The UN Secretary-General has welcomed the agreement and urged all parties to respect it, expressing hope it could open the way for further negotiations. He a

news.headline: UNESCO grants ‘enhanced protection’ to cultural heritage sites in the Middle East
news.summary: Since the Middle East war started on 28 February, several sites of major cultural significance have come under attack in Israel, Iran and Lebanon. Ensuring their protection is the task of the UN agency for education, science and culture, UNESCO.&nbsp;

news.headline: More than 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN gender equality agency reports
news.summary: The war in Gaza has inflicted a far higher toll on women and girls than in previous conflicts in the Palestinian enclave, with more than 38,000 killed by Israeli air bombardment and land military operations since Hamas-led terror attacks in Israel sparked the war in October 2023, UN Women said on Fr

news.headline: Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on
news.summary: Across war-torn Sudan, women and girls “are telling a consistent story of continued experience of danger, and risks for gender-based violence” whether when fleeing to safety or arriving at displacement camps, a senior official with the UN reproductive and sexual health agency UNFPA said on Friday.

news.headline: More than half of Haitians continue to face food crisis
news.summary: 5.8 million Haitians, or roughly 52 per cent of the population, are facing crisis levels of food insecurity, or worse. Of those, more than 1.8 million are dealing with emergency levels, which means they are exhausting their last assets and unable to meet even basic food needs.

news.headline: Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar
news.summary: Six weeks since war erupted in the Middle East, the shockwaves have spread to the Caribbean region, already pushed to the brink, amid fears of a looming El Niño-linked climate disaster.

news.headline: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
news.summary: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.

news.headline: UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow
news.summary: From election support in the Central African Republic to patrols in the disputed Abyei region, UN peacekeepers are operating in increasingly volatile environments – but shrinking resources and new threats, including drone warfare, are testing their ability to keep communities safe.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 April: UN meets over China-Russia Hormuz crisis veto as humanitarian crisis continues in Lebanon
news.summary: War in Lebanon continues amid the fragile US-Iran ceasefire amid hopes for direct talks between Israel and Beirut. Meanwhile, the UN General Assembly is debating the China-Russia Security Council veto on the crucial Strait of Hormuz crisis last month, with assembly president Annalena Baerbock saying

news.headline: The Epstein files: Rights experts demand accountability, call for probe into trafficking allegations
news.summary: UN independent human rights experts&nbsp;called on Thursday for justice and accountability for young women and girls who were trafficked systematically as part of allegations contained in the so-called Epstein files.

news.headline: ‘Sudan is an atrocities laboratory’, UN aid chief tells Berlin conference
news.summary: Foreign ministers from across the world met in Berlin on Wednesday to show support for Sudan, where a fourth year of brutal warfare has begun as humanitarian needs deepen.&nbsp;

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE:  Security fears rise in eastern DR Congo
news.summary: The UN Security Council met Wednesday over the deteriorating security situation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and wider Great Lakes region. Despite mediation efforts in Doha and Washington, regional tensions between the DRC, Rwanda, and Burundi remain acute. Follow the full live updates be

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 15 April: Civilian dangers intensify as Israel expands Lebanon evacuation orders
news.summary: Across the Middle East, tensions remain high with Israeli military operations continuing in Lebanon following direct talks between the two governments in Washington on Tuesday. More than 2,000 Lebanese have been reported killed since early March. Uncertainty also continues over the crucial Strait of

news.headline: Africa: Nearly 20 million measles deaths averted since 2000
news.summary: Measles vaccinations have saved nearly 20 million lives in Africa since the year 2000 and more than 500 million children were protected through routine immunisation, but the continent remains offtrack in the fight against vaccine-preventable diseases.&nbsp;

news.headline: Middle East conflict chokes end of supply chain as lights go out in the Pacific
news.summary: For Pacific Island countries, the Middle East crisis is not a distant geopolitical event. It is already showing up in higher fuel prices, electricity uncertainty and fears that communities sitting at the far end of global supply chains could be pushed into deeper economic insecurity.

news.headline: Tobago’s teen archery champion takes aim at sustainability targets
news.summary: Six-time Trinidad and Tobago archery champion, Anthurium Lewis, has told UN News how sport helped her overcome age barriers in environmental advocacy and how in the future it can contribute to reaching globally agreed poverty and sustainability goals.

news.headline: World failing Sudan as war enters a fourth year, UN relief chief warns
news.summary: Sudan remains the world’ s largest humanitarian and displacement crisis, UN agencies and partners said on Tuesday, calling for an end to the war between rival militaries on the eve of the three-year mark.&nbsp;

news.headline: Yemenis are ‘hanging by a thread’ top aid official warns Security Council
news.summary: Yemen must not be drawn into the escalating conflict in the Middle East, the Security Council heard on Tuesday, stressing the need for de-escalation, political progress and urgent humanitarian funding for beleaguered civilians battered by years of grinding war.&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘Time for diplomacy over escalation’ in Middle East war: Guterres
news.summary: As the war in the Middle East continues, the United Nations Secretary-General issued a passionate call for “serious negotiations” between the US and Iran to resume, warning that respect for international law “is being trampled” underfoot.&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘Now is the moment to invest’: Syria needs global backing, says UN expert
news.summary: Syria has made “remarkable progress” on transitional justice within the past year, raising hopes for accountability and recovery after more than a decade of civil conflict.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 14 April: UN chief says US-Iran ceasefire must continue, urges respect for international law
news.summary: Respect for international law is being “trampled” the UN chief warned on Tuesday, urging further negotiations between the US and Iran over ending the war and extending the fragile ceasefire as needed. Saying “there is no military solution,” he called for international navigation rights to be restore

news.headline: Fleeing Sudan's war, refugees rebuild their lives in Uganda
news.summary: On the outskirts of the Ugandan town of Biale, tents are scattered along dirt roads that give way to open fields. The Kriandongo camp sits between a shattered past and a life tentatively being rebuilt. Here, the story does not end with fleeing war. Another phase begins, one where days are measured n

news.headline: Sudan: Top UN aid official warns of ‘abandoned crisis’ as war enters fourth year
news.summary: As Sudan’s war moves into a fourth year, civilians are still being killed, displaced and subjected to widespread sexual violence, the UN’s top humanitarian official in the country warned on Monday, calling for urgent action to stop the fighting and protect civilians.

news.headline: US-Iran war: Guterres calls for continued talks, end to ceasefire violations
news.summary: The United States and Iran must continue talks aimed at ending their weeks-long war, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said on Monday, underscoring that there is no military solution to the conflict.&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘Clock is ticking’: Hormuz disruption raises fears of global food crisis
news.summary: The clock is ticking for global food systems as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz threaten to choke off the flow of fuel and crucial fertilizers needed for the next planting season – also raising the risk of higher food prices and a new wave of inflation.&nbsp;

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 13 April: US-Iran talks falter; Strait of Hormuz uncertainty deepens
news.summary: Weekend developments have raised fresh concerns worldwide after talks between the United States and Iran on Saturday ended without agreement, fuelling uncertainty and raising tensions once again. The planned US blockade targeting Iranian ports going into effect has added to fears of further escalati

news.headline: European Union support for the UN ‘more vital than ever’, Security Council hears
news.summary: A senior United Nations official on Monday hailed the European Union (EU) as “a major economic and diplomatic actor and a strong advocate of multilateralism.”&nbsp;&nbsp;

news.headline: From conflict to catwalk: Women ex-combatants weave reconciliation in Colombia
news.summary: Katerine Avella is a former combatant in Colombia’s decades-long civil war, a peace signatory and a community leader. After the guns fell silent, she created the fashion brand Ixora but, with violence returning to the region, Ms. Avella is now focusing on trying to keep the project afloat in the fac

news.headline: Lebanon airstrike casualties ‘still under the rubble’ as ambulances, hospitals face new threats
news.summary: With Lebanon still reeling from Israel’s devastating airstrikes on 8 April, UN humanitarians reported new fears of attacks on ambulances and looming food shortages in the south of the country on Friday.

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 10 April: Fear and uncertainty grow in Lebanon as conflict grinds on
news.summary: Fighting continues across parts of the Middle East, with renewed exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon. Humanitarian needs are deepening, with rising civilian casualties, mounting displacement and growing strain on services. Diplomatic efforts continue with high stakes negotiations due t

news.headline: Haiti’s freefall demands urgent global action as millions face hunger and violence
news.summary: Haiti is facing “one of the most severe and rapidly deteriorating humanitarian crises in the Western Hemisphere,” a senior UN aid official warned on Friday, underscoring the need for continued global attention to alleviate suffering there.&nbsp;

news.headline: Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a ‘horrific’ day of Israeli strikes
news.summary: The scale and speed of destruction from the wave of airstrikes in Lebanon which began just hours after the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, has left the country’s already strained health system struggling to cope, according to the World Health Organization (WHO).

news.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire
news.summary: With a US-Iran ceasefire offering a fragile glimmer of hope after weeks of conflict, violence continues to reverberate across the Middle East. Massive airstrikes in Lebanon have caused heavy civilian casualties and widespread destruction, drawing strong UN condemnation. As humanitarian needs deepen

news.headline: Kosovo mission head warns ‘mistrust’ threatens post-election stability
news.summary: Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a “delicate equilibrium” persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.

news.headline: Iran ceasefire raises hopes for reopening key Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: The announcement of a shaky two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran will, it is hoped, lead to the opening of the strategically important Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway through which one fifth of the world’s oil and gas passes.

news.headline: Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes
news.summary: The United Nations has strongly condemned airstrikes by the Israeli military across Lebanon on Wednesday which have resulted in significant casualties and destruction. &nbsp;

news.headline: ‘Entire families brutally erased’: Remembering the victims of the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda
news.summary: Thirty-two years ago, a genocidal campaign was unleashed against Rwanda’s Tutsi minority, resulting in more than one million deaths. On Tuesday, the UN is holding commemorations to ensure that the genocide is never forgotten and never repeated.

news.headline: Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies
news.summary: The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a ‘worsening’ humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by&nbsp;Hurricane Melissa last year.

news.headline: ‘We watched them die before our eyes’: Sudan health workers helpless amid medical shortages
news.summary: As violence forces tens of thousands to flee Sudan’s South Kordofan state, doctors in a key maternity hospital are facing impossible choices – with too few supplies, too many patients, and lives slipping away.

news.headline: Deminers race to keep up with military technology
news.summary: In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.&nbsp;

news.headline: Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan
news.summary: Fears are mounting for civilians caught up in Sudan’s deadly war between rival militaries as attacks intensify and humanitarian access shrinks, following a deadly airstrike on a funeral gathering in West Kordofan.

news.headline: Kidnapped and tortured on an infamous migration route to Saudi Arabia
news.summary: An Ethiopian man describes how he was tortured by human traffickers as he went in search of his nephew on a now infamous migration route from the Horn of Africa through Yemen to Saudi Arabia.

news.headline: Myanmar: WFP reports fragile recovery one year after deadly earthquake
news.summary: A “new wave of global instability is hitting Myanmar at the worst possible moment,” a UN official in the country warned on Friday, as increases in fuel, food, and fertilizer prices due to the ongoing Middle East conflict push vulnerable families closer to hunger one year after a devastating earthqua

news.headline: Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office
news.summary: More than four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the “danger is only increasing”, particularly from attack drones, a top UN human rights official warned on Thursday.

news.headline: Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians
news.summary: The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan’s Darfur has risen further, amid a “sharp increase” in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of ‘summary executions’
news.summary: Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using “unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions.”

news.headline: Ukraine violence ‘worse than ever’, Security Council hears
news.summary: More than four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, “the violence is worse than ever” and far from abating, the head of UN political and peacebuilding affairs said on Monday in a briefing to the Security Council.&nbsp;

news.headline: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
news.summary: The Security Council is met for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 13

news.headline: How Kyrgyzstan built a system to support domestic violence survivors
news.summary: Domestic violence was not something people spoke about openly in Kyrgyzstan in the 1990s following the collapse of the Soviet Union, but after a long road of dedicated efforts, there are now laws addressing family abuse, crisis centres and hotlines while human rights defenders tackle such new challe

news.headline: WHO verifies deadly hospital attack in war-torn Sudan
news.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) verified on Saturday a hospital attack in war-torn Sudan that killed 64 people.

news.headline: Forests are more than a resource: How Belarus protects its vital ecosystem
news.summary: Forests cover more than 40 per cent of Belarus. At the same time, the country recorded twice as many forest fires last year compared to the year before.

news.headline: The UN housing development which challenged 1940s’ segregated United States
news.summary: At a time when some state laws dictated where different races could live, Parkway Village, built to house some of the first UN staff in New York in 1947, led the way in eliminating racially segregated housing in the United States.

news.headline: Middle East war shockwaves ripple through Asia-Pacific fuel and supply chains
news.summary: The fallout from the war in the Middle East is rippling far beyond the Gulf, disrupting fuel supplies, shipping routes and supply chains across Asia and the Pacific, with some of the region’s most vulnerable economies already feeling the strain through rising prices, rationing and threats to jobs, f

news.headline: Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul
news.summary: Top UN officials condemned on Tuesday Pakistan’s overnight strike on a rehab centre that reportedly killed at least 400 people in Kabul, according to Taliban authorities, and injured more than 250 others.

news.headline: Deepfakes, voice cloning and weaponised AI: Global wake-up call to organised fraud
news.summary: The Sawyers from Australia were never really interested in volatile investing. As their retirement age approached, the idea of a low-risk investment for their pension seemed attractive. But one day, after clicking on a seemingly legitimate online advert that offered a reasonable risk-averse plan, th

news.headline: ‘Hope’ for Haiti’s political future as some 300 groups reportedly register for upcoming elections
news.summary: Haiti remains mired in a multidimensional crisis marked by weak institutions, political uncertainty, widespread gang violence and overwhelming humanitarian needs, but a recent new agreement by political groups offers “a moment of hope and progress for the Haitian people”, according to the UN’s most

news.headline: ‘Glimmer of hope’ in Haiti amid shifting gang frontlines
news.summary: The liberation of territory from gangs and a more “motivated and visible” police presence has provided a “glimmer of hope” for Haiti as the Caribbean island nation continues to struggle with violence, insecurity and poverty.

news.headline: Myanmar at a ‘crossroads’: The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert
news.summary: More than five years after Myanmar’s military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.

news.headline: Russia deportation of Ukraine children is crime against humanity: independent probe
news.summary: Scores of Ukrainian children are still missing after being deported far and wide across Russia and occupied territories while their families continue to search for them, human rights investigators said on Thursday.

news.headline: Venezuela’s repressive State apparatus ‘intact’ despite leadership change
news.summary: Venezuela’s repressive State apparatus remains operational despite the seizure of former President Nicolás Maduro by United States forces on 3 January, according to independent UN human rights investigators.

news.headline: UN relief chief condemns '$1 billion-a-day' cost of war in Middle East
news.summary: The UN’s emergency relief chief on Wednesday condemned the “$1 billion-a-day” cost of the war in the Middle East,&nbsp;at a time when humanitarian needs are soaring and aid funding is falling dangerously short.

news.headline: Nicaragua: Rights experts uncover State corruption fuelling repression, spying on exiles
news.summary: Nicaragua’s Government is financing the repression of its opponents through illegal misuse of public funds and targeting exiles through a transnational surveillance and intelligence network, the UN Group of Independent Human Rights Experts on Nicaragua warned in its latest report on Tuesday.

news.headline: Humanitarian crisis ‘worsening’ in Afghanistan as war erupts in the Middle East
news.summary: Crises in the region on both Afghanistan’s longest borders are undermining the country’s stability, a senior UN official warned the Security Council on Monday as concerns over Middle East crisis grow amid clashes with Pakistan and a worsening humanitarian crisis.

news.headline: Radio Begum: A rare public space for women in Afghanistan
news.summary: Every morning in Kabul, several cars make their way across the Afghan capital to pick up the producers of Radio Begum. The young women do not travel to the office on their own as moving around the city has become too complicated.

news.headline: Justice on the move: Mobile courts offer hope for communities in South Sudan
news.summary: Travelling more than 200 kilometres (124 miles) from Yambio, the capital of&nbsp;Western Equatoria State in southwestern South Sudan, a team of justice experts escorted by United Nations peacekeepers moved slowly along rough, dusty roads, determined to reach communities that have waited years fo

news.headline: ‘AI Kid of India’ urges young people to embrace technology
news.summary: 16-year-old Raul John Aju – dubbed the “AI Kid of India” at home – is a business prodigy who advises government and industry and has created several innovative AI tools.&nbsp;

news.headline: Forgotten conflict in South Sudan at ‘a dangerous point’, Türk warns
news.summary: South Sudan was the focus of debate in the UN Human Rights Council on Friday as escalating violence and political tensions – alongside a massive humanitarian emergency and war in neighbouring Sudan – threaten efforts to achieve lasting peace.&nbsp;

news.headline: Eight years of captivity: Finding freedom and healing in Ukraine
news.summary: Four years after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many people are trying to overcome deep-rooted trauma and rebuild what has been lost.&nbsp;

news.headline: Human Rights Council hears of ongoing risk of further genocidal violence in Sudan
news.summary: Nearly three years of war in Sudan have been marked by killings, rape and other violations, with risk of genocidal violence spreading, the UN Human Rights Council heard on Thursday.&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘The trauma will stay with me for life,’ says survivor of sexual violence in war-torn Ukraine
news.summary: One evening in war-ravaged southern Ukraine, a group of armed men entered Maryna’s home. She had nowhere to hide.

news.headline: Humanitarian pressures grow as Cuba continues to struggle with energy shortages
news.summary: Cuba’s humanitarian situation is worsening as fuel shortages deepen nearly a month after Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation, a senior UN official warned on Wednesday.

news.headline: South Sudan: Funding gaps threaten nearly 2 million displaced people
news.summary: Funding shortfalls are putting the lives of more than 1.9 million displaced people in South Sudan at risk amid rising humanitarian needs, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Wednesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Central African Republic enters ‘decisive’ phase after landmark elections
news.summary: Fresh from holding the most extensive elections in its history, the Central African Republic (CAR) is entering what the UN’s top envoy in the country describes as a “decisive period” in consolidating fragile peace gains, even as security challenges persist and disarmament efforts continue.

news.headline: Ukraine: ‘Use every diplomatic tool to end this war’, top UN official tells Security Council
news.summary: The international community must “use every diplomatic tool” to bring an end to the war in Ukraine, a senior UN official told the Security Council on Tuesday as Russia’s full-scale invasion entered a fifth year.
